{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8b4475bb7c6c49649d9cef58","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR\n2\t\n3\t**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n4\t\n5\tARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n6\t\n7\t- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n8\t- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n9\t- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n10\t- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n11\t- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n12\t\n13\t## Quick Start\n14\t\n15\t### Docker\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\tdocker run -d \\\n19\t  -p 9000:9000 \\\n20\t  -p 9001:9001 \\\n21\t  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n22\t  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n23\t  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n24\t  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n25\t  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n26\t  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n27\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n28\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n29\t  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\t> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n33\t\n34\t### Client Configuration\n35\t\n36\tPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n37\t\n38\t```bash\n39\t# AWS CLI\n40\taws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n41\t\n42\t# boto3 (Python)\n43\timport boto3\n44\ts3 = boto3.client('s3',\n45\t    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n46\t    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n47\t    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\n48\ts3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n49\t\n50\t# DuckDB\n51\tINSTALL httpfs;\n52\tLOAD httpfs;\n53\tSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\n54\tSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\n55\tSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\n56\tSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\t## Cost Model\n60\t\n61\t| Component | Cost |\n62\t|-----------|------|\n63\t| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n64\t| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n65\t| B2 API calls | $0 |\n66\t| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n67\t| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n68\t\n69\t## Architecture\n70\t\n71\t### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\t┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n75\t│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n76\t│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n77\t│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n78\t└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n82\t\n83\t```\n84\t┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n85\t│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n86\t│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n87\t│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n88\t└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n89\t```\n90\t\n91\t### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n92\t\n93\tDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n94\t\n95\t```\n96\tDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n97\t  │                                │                              │\n98\t  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n99\t  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n100\t  │                                │                              │\n101\t  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n102\t  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n103\t  │                                │                              │\n104\t  └─ result set                    └                              └\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t## Encryption Design\n108\t\n109\t```\n110\tMaster Key (MEK)\n111\t │  stored locally, never uploaded\n112\t │\n113\t └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n114\t                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n115\t                │\n116\t                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n117\t                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n118\t                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n119\t                                   seekable random access\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\tKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n123\t\n124\t## Security Model\n125\t\n126\t| Threat | Mitigation |\n127\t|--------|-----------|\n128\t| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n129\t| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n130\t| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n131\t| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n132\t| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n133\t| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\tARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n140\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n141\t| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n142\t| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n143\t| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n144\t| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n145\t| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n146\t| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n147\t| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n148\t| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n149\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n150\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n151\t| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n152\t| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n153\t| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n154\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n155\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n156\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n157\t\n158\t### Multi-Key Routing\n159\t\n160\tRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n161\t\n162\t```bash\n163\tARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\n164\tARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\n165\tARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\n166\tARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Multi-Credential Configuration\n170\t\n171\t```bash\n172\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\n173\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\n174\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n175\t\n176\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\n177\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\n178\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t## S3 API Coverage\n182\t\n183\t### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n184\t\n185\t| Operation | Support |\n186\t|-----------|---------|\n187\t| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n188\t| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n189\t| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n190\t| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n191\t| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n192\t| UploadPart | Full |\n193\t| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n194\t| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n195\t| ListParts | Full |\n196\t| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n197\t\n198\t### Passthrough Operations\n199\t\n200\t| Operation | Support |\n201\t|-----------|---------|\n202\t| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n203\t| DeleteObject | Full |\n204\t| DeleteObjects | Full |\n205\t| ListBuckets | Full |\n206\t| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n207\t| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n208\t| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n209\t\n210\t**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n211\t\n212\tThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n213\t\n214\t- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n215\t- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n216\t- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n217\t- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n218\t- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n219\t- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n220\t- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n221\t\n222\tInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n223\t\n224\t## Web Dashboard\n225\t\n226\tA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n227\t\n228\t```bash\n229\topen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n230\t```\n231\t\n232\tFeatures:\n233\t- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n234\t- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n235\t- Metadata cache hit rates\n236\t- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n237\t\n238\tSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n239\t\n240\t## Admin API\n241\t\n242\tKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n243\t\n244\t| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n245\t|----------|--------|-------------|\n246\t| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n247\t| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n248\t| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n249\t| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n250\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n251\t| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n252\t| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n253\t| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n254\t| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n255\t| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n256\t\n257\t## Repository Structure\n258\t\n259\t```\n260\tARMOR/\n261\t├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n262\t├── internal/\n263\t│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n264\t│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n265\t│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n266\t│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n267\t│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n268\t│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n269\t│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n270\t│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n271\t│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n272\t│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n273\t│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n274\t├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n275\t├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n276\t└── docs/\n277\t    ├── dashboard.md\n278\t    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n279\t    └── research/\n280\t```\n281\t\n282\t## Documentation\n283\t\n284\t- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n285\t- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n286\t- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n287\t- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n288\t\n289\t## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n290\t\n291\tARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n292\t\n293\t- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n294\t- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n295\t\n296\t### Building the Decrypt Tool\n297\t\n298\t```bash\n299\tgo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n300\t```\n301\t\n302\t### Usage\n303\t\n304\t#### Decrypt from B2\n305\t\n306\t```bash\n307\t# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\n308\tarmor-decrypt \\\n309\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n310\t  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n311\t  -output recovered-file.txt\n312\t\n313\t# Using MEK from environment\n314\texport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\n315\tarmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n316\t\n317\t# With verbose output\n318\tarmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n319\t```\n320\t\n321\t#### Decrypt from Local File\n322\t\n323\tFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n324\t\n325\t```bash\n326\tarmor-decrypt \\\n327\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n328\t  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n329\t  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n330\t  -output plaintext.bin\n331\t```\n332\t\n333\t### Key Requirements\n334\t\n335\t- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n336\t- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n337\t- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n338\t\n339\t### Multi-Key Support\n340\t\n341\tIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n342\t\n343\t```bash\n344\tarmor-decrypt \\\n345\t  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n346\t  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n347\t  -key-id sensitive \\\n348\t  -output recovered.txt\n349\t```\n350\t\n351\tThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n352\t\n353\t### Verification\n354\t\n355\tThe decrypt tool automatically:\n356\t\n357\t- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n358\t- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n359\t- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n360\t\n361\tExit codes:\n362\t- `0`: Success\n363\t- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n364\t\n365\t### Example Workflow\n366\t\n367\t```bash\n368\t# 1. List objects to find the target\n369\taws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n370\t\n371\t# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\n372\taws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n373\t  --bucket bucket --key file\n374\t\n375\t# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\n376\tarmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n377\t\n378\t# 4. Verify the recovered file\n379\tsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n380\t```\n381\t\n382\t## License\n383\t\n384\tMIT\n385\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"07224e6b-39e4-416b-a54b-39b7dd35adcf","uuid":"ccc171c4-9233-41fe-a1ad-5ee3f6402314","timestamp":"2026-07-09T04:13:54.093Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/README.md","content":"# ARMOR\n\n**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n\nARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n\n- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker run -d \\\n  -p 9000:9000 \\\n  -p 9001:9001 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n```\n\n> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n\n### Client Configuration\n\nPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n\n```bash\n# AWS CLI\naws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n\n# boto3 (Python)\nimport boto3\ns3 = boto3.client('s3',\n    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\ns3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n\n# DuckDB\nINSTALL httpfs;\nLOAD httpfs;\nSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\nSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\nSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\nSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n```\n\n## Cost Model\n\n| Component | Cost |\n|-----------|------|\n| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n| B2 API calls | $0 |\n| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n\nDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n\n```\nDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  └─ result set                    └                              └\n```\n\n## Encryption Design\n\n```\nMaster Key (MEK)\n │  stored locally, never uploaded\n │\n └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n                │\n                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n                                   seekable random access\n```\n\nKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n\n## Security Model\n\n| Threat | Mitigation |\n|--------|-----------|\n| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n\n## Configuration\n\nARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n\n### Multi-Key Routing\n\nRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n\n```bash\nARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\nARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\nARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\nARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n```\n\n### Multi-Credential Configuration\n\n```bash\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n```\n\n## S3 API Coverage\n\n### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n| UploadPart | Full |\n| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n| ListParts | Full |\n| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n\n### Passthrough Operations\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n| DeleteObject | Full |\n| DeleteObjects | Full |\n| ListBuckets | Full |\n| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n\n**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n\nThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n\n- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n\nInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n\n## Web Dashboard\n\nA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n```bash\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\nFeatures:\n- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n- Metadata cache hit rates\n- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n\nSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n\n## Admin API\n\nKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n\n## Repository Structure\n\n```\nARMOR/\n├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n├── internal/\n│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n└── docs/\n    ├── dashboard.md\n    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n    └── research/\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n\n## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n\nARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n\n- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n\n### Building the Decrypt Tool\n\n```bash\ngo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n```\n\n### Usage\n\n#### Decrypt from B2\n\n```bash\n# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n  -output recovered-file.txt\n\n# Using MEK from environment\nexport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\narmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n\n# With verbose output\narmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n```\n\n#### Decrypt from Local File\n\nFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n  -output plaintext.bin\n```\n\n### Key Requirements\n\n- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n\n### Multi-Key Support\n\nIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n  -key-id sensitive \\\n  -output recovered.txt\n```\n\nThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n\n### Verification\n\nThe decrypt tool automatically:\n\n- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n\nExit codes:\n- `0`: Success\n- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n\n### Example Workflow\n\n```bash\n# 1. List objects to find the target\naws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n\n# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\naws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n  --bucket bucket --key file\n\n# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\narmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n\n# 4. Verify the recovered file\nsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n```\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":385,"startLine":1,"totalLines":385}}}

All filter settings are hardcoded in Rust source code at `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`.\n10\t\n11\t---\n12\t\n13\t## 1. Core Filters Structure\n14\t\n15\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/bead_store/mod.rs:76-82`\n16\t\n17\t```rust\n18\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n19\tpub struct Filters {\n20\t    /// Only return beads assigned to this actor. `None` = no filter.\n21\t    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n22\t    /// Exclude beads that have any of these labels.\n23\t    pub exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n24\t}\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t### Filter Settings:\n28\t\n29\t| Setting | Type | Default Value | Purpose |\n30\t|---------|------|---------------|---------|\n31\t| `assignee` | `Option<String>` | `None` | Filters beads to only those assigned to a specific actor |\n32\t| `exclude_labels` | `Vec<String>` | See below | Removes beads with any of these labels from candidate list |\n33\t\n34\t---\n35\t\n36\t## 2. Default Exclude Labels\n37\t\n38\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:13`\n39\t\n40\t```rust\n41\tconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n42\t```\n43\t\n44\t### Default Excluded Labels:\n45\t\n46\t| Label | Purpose |\n47\t|-------|---------|\n48\t| `deferred` | Beads intentionally deferred for later processing |\n49\t| `human` | Beads requiring human intervention/manual review |\n50\t| `blocked` | Beads blocked by dependencies (not ready to process) |\n51\t\n52\t**Note:** These defaults are **only applied when no custom exclude_labels are provided** to `PluckStrand::new()`.\n53\t\n54\t---\n55\t\n56\t## 3. PluckStrand Configuration\n57\t\n58\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:16-21`\n59\t\n60\t```rust\n61\tpub struct PluckStrand {\n62\t    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n63\t    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n64\t    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n65\t    split_after_failures: u32,\n66\t}\n67\t```\n68\t\n69\t### Configuration Parameters:\n70\t\n71\t| Parameter | Type | Default | Purpose |\n72\t|-----------|------|---------|---------|\n73\t| `exclude_labels` | `Vec<String>` | `[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]` | Labels to filter from candidates |\n74\t| `split_after_failures` | `u32` | `3` | Consecutive failure threshold before auto-split trigger |\n75\t\n76\t---\n77\t\n78\t## 4. Runtime Filtering Logic\n79\t\n80\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:118-133`\n81\t\n82\tPluck applies multiple filter stages during evaluation:\n83\t\n84\t### Stage 1: Store Query Filters (Line 105-108)\n85\t```rust\n86\tlet filters = Filters {\n87\t    assignee: None,\n88\t    exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n89\t};\n90\t```\n91\t\n92\t### Stage 2: Defensive Label Filtering (Line 125)\n93\t```rust\n94\tcandidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n95\t```\n96\t**Purpose:** Double-checks that excluded-label beads are never presented, even if the backing store doesn't filter them.\n97\t\n98\t### Stage 3: Status/Assignee Filtering (Line 130-133)\n99\t```rust\n100\tcandidates.retain(|b| {\n101\t    !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n102\t        || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n103\t});\n104\t```\n105\t**Filters out:**\n106\t- Beads with `in_progress` status (claimed by another worker)\n107\t- `Open` beads with a stale assignee (would cause hot-loop retry failures)\n108\t\n109\t---\n110\t\n111\t## 5. Sorting Configuration\n112\t\n113\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:79-86`\n114\t\n115\t```rust\n116\tfn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n117\t    candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n118\t        a.priority\n119\t            .cmp(&b.priority)\n120\t            .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n121\t            .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n122\t    });\n123\t}\n124\t```\n125\t\n126\t### Sort Order (Deterministic):\n127\t\n128\t1. **Priority** (ASC) - Lower numbers first (P1 < P2 < P3)\n129\t2. **Created Date** (ASC) - Older beads first\n130\t3. **Bead ID** (ASC) - Lexicographic tie-breaker\n131\t\n132\t**Purpose:** Ensures all workers compute identical candidate ordering from the same queue state.\n133\t\n134\t---\n135\t\n136\t## 6. Split Trigger Configuration\n137\t\n138\t**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:141-148`\n139\t\n140\t```rust\n141\tif self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n142\t    if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n143\t        let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n144\t        if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n145\t            return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n146\t        }\n147\t    }\n148\t}\n149\t```\n150\t\n151\t### Split Logic:\n152\t\n153\t| Setting | Value | Behavior |\n154\t|---------|-------|----------|\n155\t| `split_after_failures` | `0` | Split disabled - bead returned for normal processing |\n156\t| `split_after_failures` | `> 0` | Split triggered when `failure-count:N` ≥ threshold |\n157\t\n158\t### Failure Count Extraction (Line 66-73):\n159\t```rust\n160\tfn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n161\t    bead.labels\n162\t        .iter()\n163\t        .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n164\t        .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n165\t        .max()\n166\t        .unwrap_or(0)\n167\t}\n168\t```\n169\t\n170\t**Looks for labels matching:** `failure-count:N` (e.g., `failure-count:3`)\n171\t\n172\t---\n173\t\n174\t## 7. How Filters are Applied (Execution Flow)\n175\t\n176\t```\n177\t1. Query bead store with Filters {assignee, exclude_labels}\n178\t   └─> br ready --json [--assignee <actor>]\n179\t\n180\t2. Apply defensive label filtering\n181\t   └─> Remove beads with any label in exclude_labels\n182\t\n183\t3. Apply status/assignee filtering  \n184\t   └─> Remove in_progress beads\n185\t   └─> Remove Open beads with stale assignee\n186\t\n187\t4. Sort by deterministic priority order\n188\t   └─> (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n189\t\n190\t5. Check split trigger\n191\t   └─> If failure-count >= split_after_failures: return Split\n192\t   └─> Else: return BeadFound\n193\t```\n194\t\n195\t---\n196\t\n197\t## 8. All filter settings are hardcoded in Rust source code at `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core Filters Structure\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/bead_store/mod.rs:76-82`\n\n```rust\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct Filters {\n    /// Only return beads assigned to this actor. `None` = no filter.\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    /// Exclude beads that have any of these labels.\n    pub exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n}\n```\n\n### Filter Settings:\n\n| Setting | Type | Default Value | Purpose |\n|---------|------|---------------|---------|\n| `assignee` | `Option<String>` | `None` | Filters beads to only those assigned to a specific actor |\n| `exclude_labels` | `Vec<String>` | See below | Removes beads with any of these labels from candidate list |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Default Exclude Labels\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:13`\n\n```rust\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n```\n\n### Default Excluded Labels:\n\n| Label | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|\n| `deferred` | Beads intentionally deferred for later processing |\n| `human` | Beads requiring human intervention/manual review |\n| `blocked` | Beads blocked by dependencies (not ready to process) |\n\n**Note:** These defaults are **only applied when no custom exclude_labels are provided** to `PluckStrand::new()`.\n\n---\n\n## 3. PluckStrand Configuration\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:16-21`\n\n```rust\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n```\n\n### Configuration Parameters:\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Purpose |\n|-----------|------|---------|---------|\n| `exclude_labels` | `Vec<String>` | `[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]` | Labels to filter from candidates |\n| `split_after_failures` | `u32` | `3` | Consecutive failure threshold before auto-split trigger |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Runtime Filtering Logic\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:118-133`\n\nPluck applies multiple filter stages during evaluation:\n\n### Stage 1: Store Query Filters (Line 105-108)\n```rust\nlet filters = Filters {\n    assignee: None,\n    exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n};\n```\n\n### Stage 2: Defensive Label Filtering (Line 125)\n```rust\ncandidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n```\n**Purpose:** Double-checks that excluded-label beads are never presented, even if the backing store doesn't filter them.\n\n### Stage 3: Status/Assignee Filtering (Line 130-133)\n```rust\ncandidates.retain(|b| {\n    !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n        || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n});\n```\n**Filters out:**\n- Beads with `in_progress` status (claimed by another worker)\n- `Open` beads with a stale assignee (would cause hot-loop retry failures)\n\n---\n\n## 5. Sorting Configuration\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:79-86`\n\n```rust\nfn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n    candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n        a.priority\n            .cmp(&b.priority)\n            .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n            .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n    });\n}\n```\n\n### Sort Order (Deterministic):\n\n1. **Priority** (ASC) - Lower numbers first (P1 < P2 < P3)\n2. **Created Date** (ASC) - Older beads first\n3. **Bead ID** (ASC) - Lexicographic tie-breaker\n\n**Purpose:** Ensures all workers compute identical candidate ordering from the same queue state.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Split Trigger Configuration\n\n**Location:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs:141-148`\n\n```rust\nif self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n    if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n        let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n        if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n            return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Split Logic:\n\n| Setting | Value | Behavior |\n|---------|-------|----------|\n| `split_after_failures` | `0` | Split disabled - bead returned for normal processing |\n| `split_after_failures` | `> 0` | Split triggered when `failure-count:N` ≥ threshold |\n\n### Failure Count Extraction (Line 66-73):\n```rust\nfn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n    bead.labels\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n        .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0)\n}\n```\n\n**Looks for labels matching:** `failure-count:N` (e.g., `failure-count:3`)\n\n---\n\n## 7. How Filters are Applied (Execution Flow)\n\n```\n1. Query bead store with Filters {assignee, exclude_labels}\n   └─> br ready --json [--assignee <actor>]\n\n2. Apply defensive label filtering\n   └─> Remove beads with any label in exclude_labels\n\n3. Apply status/assignee filtering  \n   └─> Remove in_progress beads\n   └─> Remove Open beads with stale assignee\n\n4. Sort by deterministic priority order\n   └─> (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n\n5. Check split trigger\n   └─> If failure-count >= split_after_failures: return Split\n   └─> Else: return BeadFound\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Filter Configuration Summary\n\n### Configurable Filters:\n\n| Filter | Configurable Via | Default Value | Location |\n|--------|------------------|---------------|----------|\n| `exclude_labels` | `PluckStrand::new()` | `[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]` | pluck.rs:13 |\n| `split_after_failures` | `PluckStrand::with_split_threshold()` | `3` | pluck.rs:39 |\n| `assignee` | `Filters.assignee` | `None` | mod.rs:79 |\n\n### Hardcoded (Non-Configurable) Filters:\n\n| Filter | Value | Purpose | Location |\n|--------|-------|---------|----------|\n| Status filter | Remove `in_progress` | Prevent claiming beads already owned | pluck.rs:131 |\n| Assignee filter | Remove `Open + assignee` | Prevent stale assignee hot-loops | pluck.rs:132 |\n| Sort order | `(priority, created_at, id)` | Deterministic ordering | pluck.rs:80-85 |\n\n---\n\n## 9. Key Implementation Notes\n\n1. **No External Configuration:** All Pluck settings are compiled into the NEEDLE binary. Changing them requires modifying source code and recompiling.\n\n2. **Defense in Depth:** Pluck applies label filtering twice:\n   - First in the store query (`Filters.exclude_labels`)\n   - Again in the strand (`candidates.retain()`)\n   - This prevents hot-loops when backends omit label data.\n\n3. **Determinism:** Same queue state → same ordering across all workers, guaranteed by sort keys including bead ID as final tie-breaker.\n\n4. **Split Behavior:** Auto-split is **enabled by default** (threshold=3). Setting threshold to 0 disables it entirely.\n\n5. **Empty Labels = Defaults:** If `PluckStrand::new(vec![])` is called with an empty vector, the default exclude labels (`deferred`, `human`, `blocked`) are automatically applied.\n\n---\n\n## 10. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d65c1b3c9ab14d679aa50322","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n4\t//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n5\t//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n8\t\n9\tuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\n10\tuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n11\t\n12\t/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\n13\tconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n14\t\n15\t/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\n16\tpub struct PluckStrand {\n17\t    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n18\t    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n19\t    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n20\t    split_after_failures: u32,\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl PluckStrand {\n24\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n25\t    ///\n26\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n27\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n28\t    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n29\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n30\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n31\t                .iter()\n32\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n33\t                .collect()\n34\t        } else {\n35\t            exclude_labels\n36\t        };\n37\t        PluckStrand {\n38\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n39\t            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n40\t        }\n41\t    }\n42\t\n43\t    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n44\t    ///\n45\t    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n46\t    /// `blocked`) is used.\n47\t    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n48\t        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n49\t            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n50\t                .iter()\n51\t                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n52\t                .collect()\n53\t        } else {\n54\t            exclude_labels\n55\t        };\n56\t        PluckStrand {\n57\t            exclude_labels: labels,\n58\t            split_after_failures,\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n65\t    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n66\t    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n67\t        bead.labels\n68\t            .iter()\n69\t            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n70\t            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n71\t            .max()\n72\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n78\t    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n79\t    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n80\t        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n81\t            a.priority\n82\t                .cmp(&b.priority)\n83\t                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n84\t                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n85\t        });\n86\t    }\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n90\timpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n91\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n92\t        \"pluck\"\n93\t    }\n94\t\n95\t    #[tracing::instrument(\n96\t        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n97\t        skip(self, store),\n98\t        fields(\n99\t            strand = \"pluck\",\n100\t            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n101\t        )\n102\t    )]\n103\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n104\t        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n105\t        let filters = Filters {\n106\t            assignee: None,\n107\t            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n108\t        };\n109\t\n110\t        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n111\t            Ok(beads) => beads,\n112\t            Err(e) => {\n113\t                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n114\t                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n115\t            }\n116\t        };\n117\t\n118\t        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n119\t        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n120\t        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n121\t        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n122\t        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n123\t        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n124\t        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n125\t        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n126\t\n127\t        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n128\t        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n129\t        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n130\t        candidates.retain(|b| {\n131\t            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n132\t                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n133\t        });\n134\t\n135\t        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n136\t        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n137\t\n138\t        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n139\t        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n140\t        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n141\t        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n142\t            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n143\t                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n144\t                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n145\t                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n146\t                }\n147\t            }\n148\t        }\n149\t\n150\t        // 6. Return result.\n151\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n152\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n153\t        } else {\n154\t            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n160\t\n161\t#[cfg(test)]\n162\tmod tests {\n163\t    use super::*;\n164\t    use assert_eq!(PluckStrand::extract_failure_count(&bead), 0);\n801\t    }\n802\t}\n803\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"07224e6b-39e4-416b-a54b-39b7dd35adcf","uuid":"b9afbed7-cbd0-4223-a740-ca89ea80491e","timestamp":"2026-07-09T04:14:16.086Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs","content":"//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n        skip(self, store),\n        fields(\n            strand = \"pluck\",\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        )\n    )]\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => beads,\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n    struct MemoryStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n                .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n            Ok(bead.labels)\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_dependency(\n            &self,\n            _blocked_id: &BeadId,\n            _blocker_id: &BeadId,\n        ) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn claim_auto(&self, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n                reason: \"claim_auto not supported in mock\".to_string(),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// A store that returns all beads from NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n is NOT claimable.\n        // The claimer would reject it every time, causing a hot loop, so we filter\n        // these beads out at the pluck stage.\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => beads,\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n\n        // 4. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            filters = ?filters,\n            \"Querying bead store for ready candidates\"\n        );\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    count = beads.len(),\n                    \"Bead store returned {} candidates\",\n                    beads.len()\n                );\n                beads\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"Bead store query failed\"\n                );\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        let before_label_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        let after_label_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_label_filter != after_label_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                excluded_count = before_label_filter - after_label_filter,\n                remaining = after_label_filter,\n                excluded_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n                \"Label filtering excluded {} beads\",\n                before_label_filter - after_label_filter\n            );\n\n            // Log each excluded bead at DEBUG level\n            let excluded_beads: Vec<_> = candidates.iter()\n                .filter(|b| b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)))\n                .map(|b| (b.id.as_ref(), b.labels.clone()))\n                .collect();\n            for (id, labels) in excluded_beads {\n                let excluded_reasons: Vec<_> = labels.iter()\n                    .filter(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l))\n                    .map(|l| l.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %id,\n                    labels = ?labels,\n                    excluded_reasons = ?excluded_reasons,\n                    \"Excluded bead due to labels\"\n                );\n            }\n\n            // Re-filter since we logged them\n            candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_label_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by label filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        let before_status_filter = candidates.len();\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n        let after_status_filter = candidates.len();\n\n        if before_status_filter != after_status_filter {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                filtered_count = before_status_filter - after_status_filter,\n                remaining = after_status_filter,\n                \"Status/assignee filtering removed {} beads\",\n                before_status_filter - after_status_filter\n            );\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\n                count = after_status_filter,\n                \"No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n            let first = &candidates[0];\n            tracing::debug!(\n                total = candidates.len(),\n                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n                first_priority = first.priority,\n                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n        }\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                    failure_count = failure_count,\n                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n                );\n\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                        failure_count = failure_count,\n                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n                    );\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n                .collect();\n            tracing::info!(\n                count = candidates.len(),\n                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"07224e6b-39e4-416b-a54b-39b7dd35adcf","uuid":"66da6998-1053-4103-9a1d-c954cf030e78"}
