Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        if !candidates.is_empty() {\n            let first = &candidates[0];\n            tracing::debug!(\n                total = candidates.len(),\n                first_bead_id = %first.id,\n                first_priority = first.priority,\n                first_created_at = %first.created_at,\n                \"Sorting {} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n        }\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                    failure_count = failure_count,\n                    threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                    split_triggered = failure_count >= self.split_after_failures,\n                    \"Checking split trigger for first candidate\"\n                );\n\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %first_candidate.id,\n                        failure_count = failure_count,\n                        threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n                        \"Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\"\n                    );\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"Split trigger disabled (threshold = 0)\");\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            tracing::debug!(\"No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            let candidate_ids: Vec<&str> = candidates.iter()\n                .map(|b| b.id.as_ref())\n                .collect();\n            tracing::info!(\n                count = candidates.len(),\n                candidates = ?candidate_ids,\n                \"Returning {} candidates for processing\",\n                candidates.len()\n            );\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }","originalFile":"//! Pluck strand: primary bead selection from the assigned workspace.\n//!\n//! Pluck handles >90% of all bead processing. It queries the bead store for\n//! unassigned, ready beads, filters by excluded labels, and sorts them in\n//! deterministic priority order: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n//!\n//! Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same candidate list.\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, Filters};\nuse crate::types::{Bead, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n/// Default labels excluded from Pluck selection when not configured.\nconst DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS: &[&str] = &[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"];\n\n/// The Pluck strand — primary work selection.\npub struct PluckStrand {\n    /// Labels to exclude from candidate selection.\n    exclude_labels: Vec<String>,\n    /// Auto-split beads after this many consecutive failures (0 = disabled).\n    split_after_failures: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PluckStrand {\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn new(exclude_labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures: 3, // default threshold\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new PluckStrand with the given exclude labels and split threshold.\n    ///\n    /// If `exclude_labels` is empty, the default set (`deferred`, `human`,\n    /// `blocked`) is used.\n    pub fn with_split_threshold(exclude_labels: Vec<String>, split_after_failures: u32) -> Self {\n        let labels = if exclude_labels.is_empty() {\n            DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_LABELS\n                .iter()\n                .map(|s| (*s).to_string())\n                .collect()\n        } else {\n            exclude_labels\n        };\n        PluckStrand {\n            exclude_labels: labels,\n            split_after_failures,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract the failure count from a bead's labels.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. Returns the count if found,\n    /// or 0 if no failure-count label exists.\n    fn extract_failure_count(bead: &Bead) -> u32 {\n        bead.labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Sort candidates in deterministic priority order.\n    ///\n    /// Sort key: `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`.\n    /// The id tie-breaker ensures identical ordering across platforms.\n    fn sort_candidates(candidates: &mut [Bead]) {\n        candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {\n            a.priority\n                .cmp(&b.priority)\n                .then_with(|| a.created_at.cmp(&b.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| a.id.as_ref().cmp(b.id.as_ref()))\n        });\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for PluckStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pluck\"\n    }\n\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"strand.pluck\",\n        skip(self, store),\n        fields(\n            strand = \"pluck\",\n            exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        )\n    )]\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        // 1. Query bead store for ready, unassigned beads.\n        let filters = Filters {\n            assignee: None,\n            exclude_labels: self.exclude_labels.clone(),\n        };\n\n        let mut candidates = match store.ready(&filters).await {\n            Ok(beads) => beads,\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Bead store error is semantically different from NoWork.\n                return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n            }\n        };\n\n        // 2. Filter: remove beads with excluded labels.\n        //    Defensive guard — store.ready() passes exclude_labels in its Filters,\n        //    but the backing CLI may not include label data in every query type.\n        //    Filtering here guarantees excluded-label beads are never presented as\n        //    candidates regardless of backend behaviour, preventing the\n        //    SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop observed when br ready --json\n        //    omits label fields for some beads.\n        candidates.retain(|b| !b.labels.iter().any(|l| self.exclude_labels.contains(l)));\n\n        // 3. Filter: remove beads that are actively in_progress (claimed by another worker)\n        //    and Open beads with a stale assignee. These are never claimable — the claimer\n        //    will reject them every time, causing a hot loop.\n        candidates.retain(|b| {\n            !(matches!(b.status, crate::types::BeadStatus::InProgress)\n                || (b.status == crate::types::BeadStatus::Open && b.assignee.is_some()))\n        });\n\n        // 4. Sort: deterministic (priority, created_at, id).\n        Self::sort_candidates(&mut candidates);\n\n        // 5. Check for split trigger: if the first candidate has accumulated\n        //    enough consecutive failures, dispatch a SPLIT instruction instead\n        //    of returning the bead for normal processing.\n        if self.split_after_failures > 0 {\n            if let Some(first_candidate) = candidates.first() {\n                let failure_count = Self::extract_failure_count(first_candidate);\n                if failure_count >= self.split_after_failures {\n                    return StrandResult::Split(Box::new(first_candidate.clone()), failure_count);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 6. Return result.\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        } else {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(candidates)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::RepairReport;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, BeadStatus, ClaimResult};\n\n    use anyhow::Result;\n    use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    /// In-memory bead store for testing.\n    struct MemoryStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MemoryStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(self.beads.clone())\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            let result: Vec<Bead> = self\n                .beads\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|b| {\n                    // Filter by assignee if set.\n                    if let Some(ref a) = filters.assignee {\n                        if b.assignee.as_ref() != Some(a) {\n                            return false;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Filter out beads with excluded labels.\n                    if b.labels.iter().any(|l| filters.exclude_labels.contains(l)) {\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    true\n                })\n                .cloned()\n                .collect();\n            Ok(result)\n        }\n\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.beads\n                .iter()\n                .find(|b| b.id == *id)\n                .cloned()\n                .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!(\"bead not found: {id}\"))\n        }\n\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented\")\n        }\n\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n\n        async fn labels(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            let bead = self.show(id).await?;\n            Ok(bead.labels)\n        }\n\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"new-bead\".to_string()))\n        }\n\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, _blocker_id: &BeadId, _blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_dependency(\n            &self,\n            _blocked_id: &BeadId,\n            _blocker_id: &BeadId,\n        ) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn claim_auto(&self, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n                reason: \"claim_auto not supported in mock\".to_string(),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// A store that returns all beads from `ready()` without any label filtering,\n    /// simulating a backend that omits label data from its ready listing.\n    struct UnfilteredStore {\n        beads: Vec<Bead>,\n    }\n\n test date\");\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id.to_string()),\n            title: format!(\"Bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority,\n            status: BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n            updated_at: Utc.from_utc_datetime(&dt),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_labels(id: &str, priority: u8, labels: Vec<&str>) -> Bead {\n        let mut bead = make_bead(id, priority, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n        bead.labels = labels.into_iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();\n        bead\n    }\n\n    fn make_bead_with_assignee(id: &str, assignee: &str) -> Bead {\n        let mut bead = make_bead(id, 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\");\n        bead.assignee = Some(assignee.to_string());\n        bead\n    }\n\n    use super::super::Strand;\n\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Sorting\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn candidates_sorted_by_priority_then_created_at() {\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead(\"low-pri\", 2, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"high-pri\", 1, \"2026-01-02 00:00:00\"),\n match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                let ids: Vec<&str> = beads.iter().map(|b| b.id.as_ref()).collect();\n                assert_eq!(ids, vec![\"high-pri-older\", \"high-pri\", \"low-pri\"]);\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn make_bead(\"bbb\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"aaa\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n                make_bead(\"ccc\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            ],\n got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Filtering\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn beads_with_excluded_labels_are_filtered() {\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"custom-excluded\", 1, vec![\"wip\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        // Custom excludes: only \"wip\" — \"deferred\" is NOT excluded.\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![\"wip\".to_string()]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                let ids: Vec<&str> = beads.iter().map(|b| b.id.as_ref()).collect();\n                assert!(ids.contains(&\"deferred-bead\"));\n                assert!(ids.contains(&\"normal-bead\"));\n                assert!(!ids.contains(&\"custom-excluded\"));\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // These tests use UnfilteredStore, which returns all beads from ready()\n    // without applying label exclusion — simulating a backend (e.g. br ready\n    // --json) that omits label data.  They verify that the strand's own\n    // defensive retain catches excluded-label beads regardless of what the\n    // store returns, preventing the SELECTING→CLAIMING→RETRYING spin loop.\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn strand_filters_excluded_labels_when_store_does_not() {\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-bead\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"normal-bead\", 1, vec![]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                assert_eq!(beads.len(), 1);\n                assert_eq!(beads[0].id.as_ref(), \"normal-bead\");\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn all_excluded_labels_returns_no_work_via_strand_filter() {\n        // When every candidate has an excluded label and the store doesn't\n        // filter them, the strand's own retain must produce an empty list\n        // and return NoWork — not BeadFound([]).  NoWork causes the worker\n        // to move to Exhausted rather than spinning the claim-retry loop.\n        let store = UnfilteredStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-1\", 1, vec![\"deferred\"]),\n                make_bead_with_labels(\"deferred-2\", 2, vec![\"deferred\", \"human\"]),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::NoWork => {}\n            other => panic!(\"expected NoWork, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn open_bead_with_stale_assignee_is_filtered() {\n        // An open bead with a leftover assignee from a previous claim is NOT claimable.\n        // The claimer would reject it every time, causing a hot loop, so we filter\n        // these beads out at the pluck stage.\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![\n                make_bead_with_assignee(\"stale-assignee\", \"worker-1\"),\n                make_bead(\"unassigned\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                assert_eq!(\n                    beads.len(),\n                    1,\n                    \"only unassigned open beads should be claimable\"\n                );\n                assert_eq!(beads[0].id.as_ref(), \"unassigned\");\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected BeadFound, got: {other:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    //    async fn empty_queue_returns_no_work() {\n        let store = MemoryStore { beads: vec![] };\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Determinism property\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn same_queue_state_produces_same_ordering() {\n        // Run twice with the same input and verify identical output.\n        let beads = vec![\n            make_bead(\"z-bead\", 2, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"a-bead\", 1, \"2026-01-03 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"m-bead\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n            make_bead(\"m-bead-2\", 1, \"2026-01-01 00:00:00\"),\n        ];\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n\n        let store1 = MemoryStore {\n            beads: beads.clone(),\n        };\n        let store2 = MemoryStore { beads };\n\n        let r1 = strand.evaluate(&store1).await;\n        let r2 = strand.evaluate(&store2).await;\n\n        let ids1: Vec<String> = match r1 {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(b) => b.iter().map(|b| b.id.to_string()).collect(),\n            _ => panic!(\"expected BeadFound\"),\n        };\n        let ids2: Vec<String> = match r2 {\n            StrandResult::BeadFound(b) => b.iter().map(|b| b.id.to_string()).collect(),\n            _ => panic!(\"expected BeadFound\"),\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(ids1, ids2, \"ordering must be deterministic\");\n        assert_eq!(ids1, vec![\"m-bead\", \"m-bead-2\", \"a-bead\", \"z-bead\"]);\n    }\n\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Name\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[test]\n    fn strand_name_is_pluck() {\n        let strand = PluckStrand::new(vec![]);\n ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    // Split trigger tests\n    // ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn split_triggered_when_failure_count_exceeds_threshold() {\n        let bead_with_failures = make_bead_with_labels(\"failing-bead\", 1, vec![\"failure-count:3\"]);\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![bead_with_failures],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::with_split_threshold(vec![], 3);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match result {\n            StrandResult::Split(bead, failure_count) => {\n                assert_eq!(bead.id.as_ref(), \"failing-bead\");\n                assert_eq!(failure_count, 3);\n            }\n            other => panic!(\"expected Split, got: {:?}\", other),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn split_not_triggered_when_failure_count_below_threshold() {\n        let bead_with_failures = make_bead_with_labels(\"failing-bead\", 1, vec![\"failure-count:2\"]);\n        let store = MemoryStore {\n            beads: vec![bead_with_failures],\n        };\n\n        let strand = PluckStrand::with_split_threshold(vec![], 3);\n        let result = strand.evaluate(&store).await;\n\n        match {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_202607091214465a91905797544a12","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_dcc38994e3b846f39a23b2b7","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-2hvf.md","content":"# Pluck Debug Logging Guide\n\n**Bead:** bf-2hvf  \n**Component:** NEEDLE Pluck Strand  \n**Source:** `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`  \n**Date:** 2026-07-09\n\n## Overview\n\nPluck now includes comprehensive debug logging that traces the entire bead selection and filtering process. All logging is done via the `tracing` crate and can be enabled at runtime.\n\n---\n\n## Debug Events\n\nPluck emits the following debug events during execution:\n\n### 1. Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold: 3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters: Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [...] }\n```\n\n### 3. Store Results\n```\nDEBUG Bead store returned {count} candidates\n  count: 42\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\nWhen beads are excluded:\n```\nDEBUG Label filtering excluded {excluded_count} beads\n  excluded_count: 5\n  remaining: 37\n  excluded_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n```\n\nFor each excluded bead:\n```\nDEBUG Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id: \"bf-1234\"\n  labels: [\"deferred\", \"priority:p2\"]\n  excluded_reasons: [\"deferred\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG Status/assignee filtering removed {filtered_count} beads\n  filtered_count: 2\n  remaining: 35\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG Sorting {total} candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n  total: 35\n  first_bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  first_priority: 1\n  first_created_at: \"2026-07-09T00:00:00Z\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Trigger Check\n```\nDEBUG Checking split trigger for first candidate\n  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  failure_count: 0\n  threshold: 3\n  split_triggered: false\n```\n\nWhen split is triggered:\n```\nINFO Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n  bead_id: \"bf-0001\"\n  failure_count: 4\n  threshold: 3\n```\n\n### 8. Result Return\nWhen candidates are found:\n```\nINFO Returning {count} candidates for processing\n  count: 35\n  candidates: [\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", \"bf-0003\", ...]\n```\n\nWhen no candidates remain:\n```\nDEBUG No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n---\n\n## Enabling Debug Logging\n\n### Method 1: Environment Variable (Recommended)\n\nSet the `RUST_LOG` environment variable to control tracing output:\n\n```bash\n# Enable Pluck DEBUG level only\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\n# Enable Pluck TRACE level (most verbose)\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle-worker\n\n# Enable DEBUG for all NEEDLE modules\nRUST_LOG=needle=debug needle-worker\n\n# Enable DEBUG for everything (very verbose)\nRUST_LOG=debug needle-worker\n```\n\n### Method 2: Tracing Filter Directives\n\nUse tracing filter syntax for fine-grained control:\n\n```bash\n# Pluck DEBUG + other crates INFO\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug,info needle-worker\n\n# Pluck TRACE + NEEDLE INFO + other crates WARN\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle=info,warn needle-worker\n```\n\n### Method 3: Log File Output\n\nTo capture debug output to a file:\n\n```bash\n# Enable DEBUG and redirect to file\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n\n# Or use a specific tracing subscriber with file output\n# (requires application support for file appenders)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Log Levels\n\n| Level | Usage | When to Use |\n|-------|-------|-------------|\n| **ERROR** | Failures that prevent operation | Store query failures |\n| **WARN** | (None currently) | - |\n| **INFO** | Significant events | Split trigger, candidate return |\n| **DEBUG** | Detailed operation trace | Filter stages, counts, decisions |\n| **TRACE** | Most granular details | (Reserved for future expansion) |\n\n---\n\n## Debug Output Examples\n\n### Example 1: Normal Operation\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] split_threshold=3 Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck filters=Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] } Querying bead store for ready candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=42 Bead store returned 42 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck excluded_count=5 remaining=37 excluded_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] Label filtering excluded 5 beads\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=37 No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck total=37 first_bead_id=\"bf-0001\" first_priority=1 first_created_at=\"2026-07-09T00:00:00Z\" Sorting 37 candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\nDEBUG strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-0001\" failure_count=0 threshold=3 split_triggered=false Checking split trigger for first candidate\nINFO strand.pluck count=37 candidates=[\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", ...] Returning 37 candidates for processing\n```\n\n### Example 2: All Beads Filtered\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck Querying bead store for ready candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=3 Bead store returned 3 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck excluded_count=3 remaining=0 Label filtering excluded 3 beads\n  bead_id=\"bf-deferred\" labels=[\"deferred\"] excluded_reasons=[\"deferred\"] Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id=\"bf-human\" labels=[\"human\"] excluded_reasons=[\"human\"] Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id=\"bf-blocked\" labels=[\"blocked\"] excluded_reasons=[\"blocked\"] Excluded bead due to labels\nDEBUG strand.pluck No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n### Example 3: Split Triggered\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 Bead store returned 1 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 No beads excluded by label filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck total=1 first_bead_id=\"bf-failing\" first_priority=2 first_created_at=\"2026-07-08T12:00:00Z\" Sorting 1 candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\nDEBUG strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-failing\" failure_count=4 threshold=3 split_triggered=true Checking split trigger for first candidate\nINFO strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-failing\" failure_count=4 threshold=3 Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n```\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting Pluck Filtering\n\n### Debugging Missing Beads\n\nIf you expect a bead to be selected but it's not appearing:\n\n1. **Enable debug logging:**\n   ```bash\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n   ```\n\n2. **Check for exclusion events:** Look for \"Excluded bead due to labels\" entries\n\n3. **Verify labels:** Check the `excluded_reasons` field to see which labels caused exclusion\n\n4. **Check status/assignee:** Look for status filtering messages\n\n### Debugging Empty Queue\n\nWhen Pluck returns NoWork but you expect beads:\n\n1. **Check initial count:** \"Bead store returned N candidates\"\n   - If 0: No beads in ready state\n   - If >0: Beads are being filtered out\n\n2. **Track filtering stages:**\n   - \"Label filtering excluded N beads\"\n   - \"Status/assignee filtering removed N beads\"\n\n3. **Verify exclude_labels:** Check if default excludes (`deferred`, `human`, `blocked`) apply\n\n### Debugging Split Behavior\n\nTo understand why a bead is (or isn't) being split:\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n```\n\nLook for the \"Checking split trigger\" event which shows:\n- `failure_count`: Current failure count from labels\n- `threshold`: Configured split threshold\n- `split_triggered`: Whether split will occur\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Tracing Instrumentation\n\nPluck uses `tracing::instrument` for automatic span creation:\n\n```rust\n#[tracing::instrument(\n    name = \"strand.pluck\",\n    skip(self, store),\n    fields(\n        strand = \"pluck\",\n        exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n    )\n)]\n```\n\n### Span Fields\n\nAll Pluck events include these fields:\n- `strand`: \"pluck\" - identifies the strand type\n- `exclude_labels`: List of labels being filtered\n- `split_threshold`: Failure count threshold for auto-split\n\n### Event Types\n\n- **macro:** `tracing::debug!()` - Detailed diagnostic information\n- **macro:** `tracing::info!()` - Significant operational events\n- **macro:** `tracing::error!()` - Failure events\n\n---\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **Pluck Filter Configurations:** See `bf-1jwl.md` for complete filter documentation\n- **Pluck exclude_labels:** See `bf-ogec.md` for exclude_labels extraction\n- **NEEDLE Logging:** Check NEEDLE project docs for global logging configuration\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **Debug logging mechanism identified and documented**\n- Tracing-based logging implemented\n- Environment variable configuration documented\n- Multiple log levels available (DEBUG, INFO, ERROR)\n\n✅ **Command or config to enable debug mode verified**\n- `RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug` enables Pluck DEBUG\n- `RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace` enables maximum verbosity\n- Works with standard tracing subscriber\n\n✅ **Debug output shows filtering logic execution**\n- Store query results logged\n- Each filtering stage (label, status/assignee) emits counts\n- Individual excluded beads logged with reasons\n- Sort operation logged with first candidate details\n- Split trigger check logged with decision\n- Final result (NoWork/BeadFound/Split) logged\n\n---\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\nPossible improvements for future work:\n\n1. **Histogram Metrics:** Track filtering statistics over time\n2. **Structured JSON Output:** Emit logs in JSON format for parsing\n3. **Per-Bead Timings:** Add timing information for each filtering stage\n4. **Filter Decision Logs:** Emit specific reason for each bead's inclusion/exclusion\n\n---\n\n**Status:** ✅ Complete - Pluck debug logging implemented and Result Return\nWhen candidates are found:\n```\nINFO Returning {count} candidates for processing\n  count: 35\n  candidates: [\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", \"bf-0003\", ...]\n```\n\nWhen no candidates remain:\n```\nDEBUG No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n---\n\n## Enabling Debug Logging\n\n### Method 1: Environment Variable (Recommended)\n\nSet the `RUST_LOG` environment variable to control tracing output:\n\n```bash\n# Enable Pluck DEBUG level only\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\n# Enable Pluck TRACE level (most verbose)\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle-worker\n\n# Enable DEBUG for all NEEDLE modules\nRUST_LOG=needle=debug needle-worker\n\n# Enable DEBUG for everything (very verbose)\nRUST_LOG=debug needle-worker\n```\n\n### Method 2: Tracing Filter Directives\n\nUse tracing filter syntax for fine-grained control:\n\n```bash\n# Pluck DEBUG + other crates INFO\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug,info needle-worker\n\n# Pluck TRACE + NEEDLE INFO + other crates WARN\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle=info,warn needle-worker\n```\n\n### Method 3: Log File Output\n\nTo capture debug output to a file:\n\n```bash\n# Enable DEBUG and redirect to file\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n\n# Or use a specific tracing subscriber with file output\n# (requires application support for file appenders)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Log Levels\n\n| Level | Usage | When to Use |\n|-------|-------|-------------|\n| **ERROR** | Failures that prevent operation | Store query failures |\n| **WARN** | (None currently) | - |\n| **INFO** | Significant events | Split trigger, candidate return |\n| **DEBUG** | Detailed operation trace | Filter stages, counts, decisions |\n| **TRACE** | Most granular details | (Reserved for future expansion) |\n\n---\n\n## Debug Output Examples\n\n### Example 1: Normal Operation\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] split_threshold=3 Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck filters=Filters { assignee: None, exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] } Querying bead store for ready candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=42 Bead store returned 42 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck excluded_count=5 remaining=37 excluded_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] Label filtering excluded 5 beads\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=37 No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck total=37 first_bead_id=\"bf-0001\" first_priority=1 first_created_at=\"2026-07-09T00:00:00Z\" Sorting 37 candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\nDEBUG strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-0001\" failure_count=0 threshold=3 split_triggered=false Checking split trigger for first candidate\nINFO strand.pluck count=37 candidates=[\"bf-0001\", \"bf-0002\", ...] Returning 37 candidates for processing\n```\n\n### Example 2: All Beads Filtered\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck Querying bead store for ready candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=3 Bead store returned 3 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck excluded_count=3 remaining=0 Label filtering excluded 3 beads\n  bead_id=\"bf-deferred\" labels=[\"deferred\"] excluded_reasons=[\"deferred\"] Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id=\"bf-human\" labels=[\"human\"] excluded_reasons=[\"human\"] Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id=\"bf-blocked\" labels=[\"blocked\"] excluded_reasons=[\"blocked\"] Excluded bead due to labels\nDEBUG strand.pluck No candidates remaining after filtering, returning NoWork\n```\n\n### Example 3: Split Triggered\n```bash\n$ RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n\nDEBUG strand.pluck Pluck strand evaluation starting\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 Bead store returned 1 candidates\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 No beads excluded by label filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck count=1 No beads excluded by status/assignee filter\nDEBUG strand.pluck total=1 first_bead_id=\"bf-failing\" first_priority=2 first_created_at=\"2026-07-08T12:00:00Z\" Sorting 1 candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\nDEBUG strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-failing\" failure_count=4 threshold=3 split_triggered=true Checking split trigger for first candidate\nINFO strand.pluck bead_id=\"bf-failing\" failure_count=4 threshold=3 Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n```\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting Pluck Filtering\n\n### Debugging Missing Beads\n\nIf you expect a bead to be selected but it's not appearing:\n\n1. **Enable debug logging:**\n   ```bash\n   RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n   ```\n\n2. **Check for exclusion events:** Look for \"Excluded bead due to labels\" entries\n\n3. **Verify labels:** Check the `excluded_reasons` field to see which labels caused exclusion\n\n4. **Check status/assignee:** Look for status filtering messages\n\n### Debugging Empty Queue\n\nWhen Pluck returns NoWork but you expect beads:\n\n1. **Check initial count:** \"Bead store returned N candidates\"\n   - If 0: No beads in ready state\n   - If >0: Beads are being filtered out\n\n2. **Track filtering stages:**\n   - \"Label filtering excluded N beads\"\n   - \"Status/assignee filtering removed N beads\"\n\n3. **Verify exclude_labels:** Check if default excludes (`deferred`, `human`, `blocked`) apply\n\n### Debugging Split Behavior\n\nTo understand why a bead is (or isn't) being split:\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle-worker\n```\n\nLook for the \"Checking split trigger\" event which shows:\n- `failure_count`: Current failure count from labels\n- `threshold`: Configured split threshold\n- `split_triggered`: Whether split will occur\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Tracing Instrumentation\n\nPluck uses `tracing::instrument` for automatic span creation:\n\n```rust\n#[tracing::instrument(\n    name = \"strand.pluck\",\n    skip(self, store),\n    fields(\n        strand = \"pluck\",\n        exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n    )\n)]\n```\n\n### Span Fields\n\nAll Pluck events include these fields:\n- `strand`: \"pluck\" - identifies the strand type\n- `exclude_labels`: List of labels being filtered\n- `split_threshold`: Failure count threshold for auto-split\n\n### Event Types\n\n- **macro:** `tracing::debug!()` - Detailed diagnostic information\n- **macro:** `tracing::info!()` - Significant operational events\n- **macro:** `tracing::error!()` - Failure events\n\n---\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **Pluck Filter Configurations:** See `bf-1jwl.md` for complete filter documentation\n- **Pluck exclude_labels:** See `bf-ogec.md` for exclude_labels extraction\n- **NEEDLE Logging:** Check NEEDLE project docs for global logging configuration\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **Debug logging mechanism identified and documented**\n- Tracing-based logging implemented\n- Environment variable configuration documented\n- Multiple log levels available (DEBUG, INFO, ERROR)\n\n✅ **Command or config to enable debug mode verified**\n- `RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug` enables Pluck DEBUG\n- `RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace` enables maximum verbosity\n- Works with standard tracing subscriber\n\n✅ **Debug output shows filtering logic execution**\n- Store query results logged\n- Each filtering stage (label, status/assignee) emits counts\n- Individual excluded beads logged with reasons\n- Sort operation logged with first candidate details\n- Split trigger check logged with decision\n- Final result (NoWork/BeadFound/Split) logged\n\n---\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\nPossible timing information for each filtering stage\n4. **Filter Decision Logs:** Emit specific reason for each bead's inclusion/exclusion\n\n---\n\n**Status:** ✅ Complete - Pluck debug logging implemented
